content.text: The system shall display a “Risk Assessment chart” button in the Rules and Action page header.
a “Risk Assessment Chart” button in the Rules and Action page
I can access the risk configuration panel.
I am on the Rules and Action page,
the header must display a button labeled “Risk Assessment Chart”.
When the user clicks “Risk assessment chart”, a centred modal titled “Risk Assessment Chart” shall open over the Rules and Action page with a background overlay.
the chart to open in a centered modal
I can configure risk settings clearly.
the Rules & Action page is loaded,
a centered modal opens with title “Risk Assessment Chart”
The modal shall display a brief helper text below the title: e.g. “Set severity levels and probability thresholds for each category.”
helper text under the modal title
I understand the purpose of the chart.
helper text appears under the title, e.g.:
“Set severity levels and probability thresholds for each category.”
The modal shall have a Close (X) icon in the top-right corner. Clicking it shall close the modal without saving unsaved changes.
I can exit the modal without saving.
a Close (X) icon appears in the top-right.

5.title: Initial View Mode + Edit Changes Button

content.text: On initial load, all numeric fields in the chart shall be view-only (greyed / non-editable) and the primary button at the bottom-right shall be labelled “Edit Changes.”
the chart to open in view-only mode
all numeric fields (severity scale + matrix values) are non-editable.
Clicking “Edit Changes” shall switch the modal into edit mode, making all Min/Max fields editable and changing the primary button label to “Save Changes.”
I can modify severity and matrix values.
all Min/Max numeric fields become editable,
the primary button label changes to “Save Changes.”
The section “Severity Scale (1–9)” shall display three color-coded categories in a row: Red, Amber, and Green, each with two numeric inputs: From and To.
I can configure Red, Amber, and Green ranges.
it shows three color-coded categories horizontally:
Each Severity Scale input shall accept only integer values from 1 to 9; non-numeric or out-of-range entries shall be prevented or flagged with inline validation.
the severity scale inputs to allow only valid integers
I try to edit a severity input,
only integer values between 1 and 9 are allowed.
non-numeric, out-of-range, or decimal entries must be:

9.title: Validate From ≤ To for Each Severity Band

content.text: The system shall validate that for each severity band, from ≤ To; if not, show inline error text under the field (e.g., “From value cannot be greater than to”).
the band definition is always valid.
“From value cannot be greater than To.”
The system shall prevent overlapping or duplicate ranges between Red, Amber, and Green (e.g., the same number cannot fall into two colours). If overlap occurs, an inline error shall appear.
the system to block overlapping definitions
each value from 1–9 belongs to exactly one color.
any number overlaps between two colors (e.g., Red 1–3 & Amber 3–5),

The section “Probability & Consequence Matrix” shall be displayed as a grid with rows for High, Moderate, Low, Minimal (probability) and columns for Minor, Significant, Critical, Catastrophic (consequence).
I can configure risk band values.
the Probability & Consequence Matrix appears with:

12.title: Min/Max Inputs Inside Each Matrix Cell

content.text: Each cell in the Probability & Consequence Matrix shall contain two numeric inputs Min and Max to define the risk score band for that probability–consequence combination.
each matrix cell to have numeric inputs
I can define the risk score band for each combination.
All matrix Min/Max inputs shall accept only integer values (no decimals, text, or negative numbers); invalid entries shall be blocked or flagged inline.
clean numeric validation in each matrix field
I edit a Min or Max field inside the matrix,
Invalid entries are blocked or show inline validation.

14.title: Validate Min ≤ Max for Every Matrix Cell

content.text: For every matrix cell, the system shall validate Min ≤ Max; if violated, an inline error shall appear.
validation ensuring Min is never greater than Max
I am editing a Probability–Consequence matrix cell,
I enter values where Min > Max,
the system displays an inline error message under that cell,
e.g., “Min value cannot be greater than Max.”
the Save Changes action is blocked.
Fields marked with an asterisk (*) in the modal (e.g., probability rows) shall be mandatory. If any required field is empty in edit mode, show inline error “Required field” and prevent save.
the matrix is always fully defined.
fields marked with an asterisk (*) are required,
any required field is left empty in edit mode,
Save Changes is prevented until all required fields are completed.
The Note section at the bottom of the modal shall display static instructional text (1.“set severity as per your experience by number 1-9 for green, amber, red
2. all required fields must be filled out. Please review your entries and complete the necessary information.”) This text is non-editable.
clear instructional text displayed at the bottom
I understand how severity and required fields work.
the Risk Assessment Chart modal is open,
a Note section appears at the bottom containing the static non-editable text:
“Set severity as per your experience by number 1–9 for green, amber, red.”
“All required fields must be filled out. Please review your entries and complete the necessary information.”
users cannot edit or modify this note.
Clicking Cancel in the bottom-left of the modal shall close the modal and discard any unsaved changes made in the current edit session.
a Cancel button to discard edits
I can exit without saving accidental modifications.
all unsaved edits from the current session are discarded
no changes are saved to the backend.

Clicking “Save Changes” shall display a confirmation pop-up of “Confirm changes?” and text below – “This update will affect all modules, inspections, and rules that reference this chart.” “Proceed only if you want these changes applied system wide.”
I understand the system-wide impact of changes.
a confirmation popup appears titled “Confirm changes?”And contains the text:
“This update will affect all modules, inspections, and rules that reference this chart. Proceed only if you want these changes applied system wide.”

19.title: Save, Close Modal, and Show Success Toast

content.text: After Clicking Apply Changes, the modal shall close automatically and the user shall be returned to the Rules and Action list screen and a green toast notification shall appear at the top-right of the Rules and Action screen with message “Risk assessment chart updated successfully” and a success icon.
successful updates to be saved and acknowledged
I know changes have been applied.
I am on the confirmation popup,
Saves the updated severity and matrix configuration
Closes the Risk Assessment Chart modal
Returns me to the Rules & Action list screen
Displays a green toast notification at top-right:

20.title: Show Latest Saved Values When Reopening

content.text: On re-opening the Risk Assessment Chart, all fields shall show the latest saved values (both Severity Scale and Probability & Consequence Matrix).
see the most recently saved chart values
I always work with the latest configuration.
the user reopens the Risk Assessment Chart,
these values appear in view-only mode until the user chooses Edit Changes.
